trim_reasons: Spread of -12.5 creates genuine blowout game-script risk: even after Pick's blowout penalty, MIN as a heavy favorite means Randle — a secondary scorer with moderate volatility and a post-miss hit rate of only 69% — could see Q4 minutes compressed if the game is decided early. The min_floor of 28.8 vs a 33.4 average leaves meaningful downside if rest/garbage-time pulls him before he reaches the threshold.

Spread of -11.5 is a structural blowout risk for a player whose min_floor (24.8) already sits near the fragility threshold, and Kawhi's known load-management history makes early exits in comfortable wins a real possibility. REB is confirmed slump-persistent and the moderate volatility tag means a single low-minutes game — entirely plausible given the lopsided spread — produces a miss even at T4.

A -11.5 spread creates a genuine blowout scenario where Kawhi's known load-management protocols risk an early exit; his min_floor of 24.8 is dangerously close to the fragility threshold for an AST prop, and per the historical audit, AST props mechanically cap when minutes are compressed. The post-miss AST hit rate of 80% combined with moderate volatility and a 24.8 min_floor means a single managed-rest exit — highly plausible in a double-digit blowout — directly collapses assist accumulation in a way the confidence adjustment may not fully capture.

VOLATILE tag on a PTS pick is the exact combination flagged in the historical audit (Brandon Miller T15 iron_floor + VOLATILE missed badly); while Durant is stepped down to T15 with confidence at 70%, the VOLATILE distribution means the floor is a statistical pattern rather than a guarantee, and a cold-FG night — already elevated by the FG_MARGIN_THIN signal — could produce a miss well below threshold as seen in prior volatile scorers.

min_floor of 24.8 sits exactly at the fragility threshold for a PTS T15+ pick — a single low-minutes game is within normal variance and produces a miss. The post-miss hit rate of 69% is below average, meaning bounce-back momentum is not a reliable safety net here.

A -17.5 spread is the most extreme blowout scenario on the slate: historical audit explicitly identifies that AST props for star players in blowout-trending games are mechanically capped when minutes compress, and SGA's min_floor of 25.8 against a 32.1 average means meaningful rest-minutes exposure. The -10% blowout AST confidence penalty was applied, but at -17.5 the structural risk of a managed early exit — where SGA logs ~25 minutes and assist accumulation is fully capped — likely exceeds what the single penalty captures; this is the exact Wembanyama pattern (comfortable win, compressed minutes, missed AST) at an even more extreme spread.
